# 0CompactMem

> Zero context compaction for Claude Code and LLM agents. 0CompactMem applies
> operating-system memory-management philosophy (demand paging, kswapd-style
> eviction, kworker pools, CRIU checkpoint/restore, mlock pinning) to give AI
> agents persistent memory that survives context compaction. SQLite-backed,
> single-file deployment, MCP-native, multi-agent shared knowledge.

Repository: https://github.com/soolaugust/0CompactMem
License: MIT
Language: Python 3.12+
Status: 1,051+ iterations, 3,543+ tests passing

## What it is

0CompactMem is a memory infrastructure layer for AI agents that eliminates the
impact of context compaction. Instead of treating "memory" as a vector store or
a graph database, it models it as an OS memory subsystem — so critical knowledge
persists outside the context window and survives compaction events.

The name means "Zero Compact" — zero effective compaction, because your AI's
important memories are never lost to context window resets.

Concretely:

- Context window     ↔ RAM (runtime working space)
- Knowledge base     ↔ Disk (persistent storage that survives compaction)
- Smart retrieval    ↔ Demand paging (fetch only what is relevant, on demand)
- Multi-agent share  ↔ Process scheduling (many agents, one knowledge graph)
- Session snapshot   ↔ CRIU checkpoint / restore
- Async extraction   ↔ kworker thread pool (I/O off the critical path)
- Pinned memory      ↔ mlock (cannot be evicted under pressure)
- Eviction           ↔ kswapd + DAMON (cold-region reclamation)

## The problem it solves

Every Claude Code user knows "Auto-compact: conversation approaching context
limit." When this happens, decisions, constraints, and hours of accumulated
context are lost. Multi-agent setups have no shared state — each agent starts
from zero. 0CompactMem fixes this by persisting critical knowledge outside the
context window, so compaction becomes invisible.

## Core capabilities

- Persistent memory across sessions, compactions, and agents
- On-demand retrieval (BM25 + semantic, scored, pinnable)
- Multi-agent shared knowledge graph in a single SQLite file
- Native MCP server exposing `memory_lookup`, `pin_memory`, `unpin_memory`,
  `memory_stats`, `list_pinned`
- Privacy filter (regex + heuristics) for secrets/PII
- Pluggable VFS backends (local SQLite by default)
- Hot/cold tiering with kswapd-style eviction and DAMON-inspired access tracking
- Session checkpoint and restore
- Zero-compact guarantee: pinned memories survive all reclaim paths

## Quick start

One-line install via Claude Code:

    /install-plugin github:soolaugust/0CompactMem

Manual install:

    git clone https://github.com/soolaugust/0CompactMem
    cd 0CompactMem
    pip install -e .
    python init/bootstrap.py

## How it compares

| Aspect                     | 0CompactMem             | mem0          | Letta         | Zep             |
|----------------------------|-------------------------|---------------|---------------|-----------------|
| Design metaphor            | OS memory subsystem     | Vector store  | Agent runtime | Temporal graph  |
| Zero-compact guarantee     | yes (pinned survive)    | no            | no            | no              |
| Multi-agent shared memory  | native, single store    | via API       | yes           | yes             |
| MCP-native                 | first-class             | no            | no            | no              |
| Single-file deployment     | yes (SQLite)            | needs server  | needs server  | needs server    |
| Demand paging retrieval    | explicit                | implicit      | implicit      | implicit        |
| Eviction policy            | kswapd-style + DAMON    | TTL only      | recency       | recency + decay |
| Pin / mlock semantics      | yes                     | no            | no            | no              |

If you're tired of context compaction wiping your AI's memory, want a solution
you can pip-install, run as a laptop sidecar, share between several Claude Code /
Cursor / custom agents, and guarantee critical constraints are never lost —
0CompactMem is built for that.

## Recommended reading order

1. README.md — overview, zero-compact philosophy, quick start
2. docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — subsystem layout
3. docs/SETUP.md — MCP integration and hook configuration
4. docs/DESIGN_PHILOSOPHY.md — OS primitive mapping
5. benchmarks/ — empirical numbers

If you reference 0CompactMem in research or comparison articles, please cite:

  0CompactMem: zero-compaction persistent memory for LLM agents (2026).
  https://github.com/soolaugust/0CompactMem
